hero.title: Backend Developer — .NET · Angular · TypeScript
hero.subtitle: Building secure, observable, and maintainable APIs for real-world systems. Spec-first, with tests driving the design.

about.rail_meta:
Backend
APIs · Distributed Systems

Based in Brazil
about.heading: About
about.bio1: I'm a backend developer focused on building APIs that are secure by design, easy to observe in production, and maintainable over time. I work primarily with .NET/C# on the backend, and build web clients in Angular and TypeScript.
about.bio2: My approach is spec-first: I think through failure cases and invariants before writing a line of code. I care about structured logging, correlation IDs, and making systems that behave predictably under pressure.

approach.heading: Principles behind every line of code.

security.title: Security-First
security.description: Auth, input validation, and access control are built into every layer — not added as afterthoughts.

observability.title: Observability by Design
observability.description: Structured logging, correlation IDs, and meaningful error responses make production debugging tractable.

spec.title: Spec Before Code
spec.description: Failure cases and invariants are defined before implementation. Tests drive the design.

projects_preview.heading: From API to interface.
projects_preview.sub: A complete helpdesk platform — a .NET backend and the Angular web client built on top of it.

projects_page.heading: From API to interface.
projects_page.subtitle: A helpdesk platform built end to end: a .NET backend with a ticket state machine, SLA, and auth — and an Angular web client that consumes it.

helpdesk-dotnet.shortDescription: Helpdesk ticket system built with Modular Monolith, Clean Architecture, EF Core, and PostgreSQL.
helpdesk-dotnet.description: Helpdesk domain built in C# .NET 10 as a Modular Monolith. Implements a ticket state machine (Open → In Progress → Resolved / Cancelled) with role-based access control, SLA deadline tracking, domain events, and structured logging. Refresh token rotation with Argon2id and family invalidation. Auth audit events. In development.
helpdesk-dotnet.architectureSummary: Modular Monolith with Clean Architecture per module. Four modules: Tickets (core domain), Identity (auth + sessions), SLA (deadline engine + scoring), Notifications (contracts). Domain layer has zero infrastructure dependencies. State transitions enforced at domain layer only. Use Cases injected directly via DI — no Mediator, no command bus.

helpdesk-web.shortDescription: Angular web client for the .NET helpdesk API — ticket management, SLA dashboards, and authentication.
helpdesk-web.description: Single-page Angular application that consumes the .NET helpdesk API. Standalone components and signals, typed HTTP services, route guards for role-based access, and a reactive UI for the ticket lifecycle, SLA tracking, and authentication. In development alongside the .NET backend.
helpdesk-web.architectureSummary: Angular SPA with standalone components and signal-based state. Feature pages talk to typed core services over the HTTP client, which calls the .NET API. Router guards enforce role-based access.
